name: container-management
description: Create, manage, and optimize LXC containers in Proxmox. Control container lifecycle, manage resources, and coordinate container deployments across nodes.
capabilities: List containers on specific nodes with current status
Start, stop, reboot, and gracefully shutdown containers
Create new LXC containers with basic or advanced configuration
Clone existing containers for testing and deployment
Monitor container performance metrics and resource usage
Modify container resource allocation for CPU, memory, and storage
author: DataKnifeAI
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing no executable code. The static analyzer produced false positives by misinterpreting JSON property values and markdown tool names as security patterns. Both files (skill-report.json and SKILL.md) are pure documentation with no scripts, network calls, or file operations. The skill-report.json explicitly marks risk_level as 'safe' and is_blocked as false.
valueStatement: Proxmox container management involves complex GUI operations and manual coordination. This skill simplifies LXC container operations with intuitive commands for lifecycle management, monitoring, and resource optimization across your Proxmox cluster.
userTitle: Manage LXC containers in Proxmox environments
limitations: Requires Proxmox VE environment with LXC support enabled
Cannot manage Docker containers or Kubernetes clusters
Limited to Proxmox node operations within configured cluster

useCases.title: Automate container deployments
useCases.description: Streamline container creation and management across Proxmox nodes without manual GUI operations

useCases.title: Monitor container health
useCases.description: Track container status, resource usage, and performance metrics for proactive infrastructure maintenance

useCases.title: Create test environments
useCases.description: Quickly provision isolated LXC containers for testing applications in standardized environments

promptTemplates.title: List containers
promptTemplates.scenario: Check all containers on a node
promptTemplates.prompt: List all containers on the worker node with their current status and resource usage

promptTemplates.title: Create container
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new LXC container with 2 CPU cores, 4GB RAM, and Ubuntu 22.04 template

promptTemplates.title: Monitor resources
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me the resource usage and performance metrics for container 101

promptTemplates.title: Clone for testing
promptTemplates.prompt: Clone container 102 to create a test environment with hostname test-app

bestPractices: Monitor container resource usage regularly to prevent over-allocation and performance degradation
Use graceful shutdown commands instead of immediate stops to avoid data corruption
Document container purposes and configurations for team visibility and troubleshooting
antiPatterns: Creating containers without resource limits can impact node performance and stability
Ignoring container logs makes troubleshooting difficult when issues arise
Running unnecessary services in containers wastes resources and increases attack surface

faq.question: Which Proxmox versions are compatible?
faq.answer: This skill works with Proxmox VE 6.x and later versions that support LXC containers.

faq.question: Can I manage Docker containers with this skill?
faq.answer: No, this skill is specifically for LXC containers in Proxmox environments only.

faq.question: Does it integrate with existing Proxmox clusters?
faq.answer: Yes, it operates on any node in your Proxmox cluster with appropriate API permissions.

faq.question: Is my container data safe during operations?
faq.answer: Yes, all operations use Proxmox APIs which maintain data integrity during container changes.

faq.question: What if a container fails to start?
faq.answer: Check container logs and resource allocation, then use reboot or diagnostic commands to recover.

faq.question: How does this compare to the Proxmox web UI?
faq.answer: Provides faster CLI-based management with automation capabilities for repetitive operational tasks.
